title: Anthropic created a test marketplace for agent-on-agent commerce
description: In a recent experiment, Anthropic created a classified marketplace where AI agents represented both buyers and sellers, striking real deals for real goods and real money.

title: Anthropic created a test marketplace for agent-on-agent commerce
description: In a recent experiment, Anthropic created a classified marketplace where AI agents represented both buyers and sellers, striking real deals for real goods and real money.

title: Maine’s governor vetoes data center moratorium
description: L.D. 307 would have imposed the country’s first statewide moratorium on new data centers — lasting, in this case, until November 1, 2027.

title: OpenAI CEO apologizes to Tumbler Ridge community
description: In a letter to the residents of Tumbler Ridge, Canada, OpenAI CEO Sam Altman said he is “deeply sorry” that his company failed to alert law enforcement about the suspect in a recent mass shooting.

title: Why Cohere is merging with Aleph Alpha
description: Canadian AI startup Cohere is taking over Germany-based Aleph Alpha with support from Lidl’s owner, Schwarz Group. With the blessing of their governments, the companies intend to offer a sovereign alternative to enterprises in an AI landscape dominated by American players.

title: Why Tokyo is the most important tech destination of 2026
description: SusHi Tech Tokyo 2026 has four tightly defined technology domains, each backed by live demonstrations, dedicated exhibit floors, and sessions featuring the people actually building and funding these technologies globally.

title: Nike cuts 1,400 jobs across tech, operations
description: The majority of the layoffs are in the activewear giant’s tech department as it consolidates its footprint and leans into automation.

title: AI citation tracking: How to track (and grow) AI engine citations
description: AI search engine citation tracking helps measure brand visibility and authority in AI-powered search results. As AI-powered search experiences reshape how people discover information, evaluate vendors, and build shortlists, visibility inside AI answers is no longer a vanity metric. If AI engines aren’t citing your brand, you’re missing influence at the exact moment buyers are forming opinions.

title: Three reasons why DeepSeek’s new model matters
description: On Friday, Chinese AI firm DeepSeek released a preview of V4, its long-awaited new flagship model. Notably, the model can process much longer prompts than its last generation, thanks to a new design that helps it handle large amounts of text more efficiently. Like DeepSeek’s previous models, V4 is open source, meaning it is available…

title: Sports guidance to brands from three sources: Keep the fan top of mind and you might just win
description: Agencies are hiring talent from the big leagues, crystallizing sport-related efforts into full-fledged business opportunities, while others look for the danger in tapping AI in too many places.

title: [AINews] GPT 5.5 and OpenAI Codex Superapp
description: Spud lives!

title: Canadian, German AI Startups Join Forces to Challenge US Dominance
description: The partners hope to provide an AI stack that prioritizes regional independence and regulatory compliance.

title: GPT-5.5 Boasts Coding Advancements, But Falls Short of Opus 4.7
description: While OpenAI has improved the model's coding and tool use ability, it still has room to improve in areas where Anthropic dominates.

title: The AI Race Is Becoming an Infrastructure Contest
description: As AI vendors commit billions to compute, power and global capacity, tech giants rush to build infrastructure ahead of proven demand.

title: Mastercard: Revenue growth, emissions reduction can coincide
description: The card network reduced its corporate carbon footprint again last year, even as revenue grew, according to an Earth Day blog post this week.
